stage.name: XRPL Deep Dive
stage.description: RPCA consensus, trust lines, native DEX + AMM, pathfinding, ODL, and why OnlyCrypto runs on XRPL.

elements.content: RPCA Consensus
Ripple Protocol Consensus Algorithm — how XRP reaches finality

3-5 seconds
Finality

~150 UNL
Validators

How RPCA Works

Each validator has a Unique Node List (UNL) — trusted validators it consults (not all validators globally)

Round 1: each validator proposes candidate transactions and broadcasts position

Round 2: validators share positions, converge on 80%+ supermajority agreement (SuperMajority threshold)

Finality: once 80%+ UNL agree on a ledger, it closes — no forks, no rollbacks possible

vs Nakamoto Consensus

RPCA achieves finality in 3-5 seconds vs Bitcoin's probabilistic 60-minute finality

No mining: no energy expenditure — validators run on commodity hardware, ~99.99% less energy than PoW

Censorship risk: if 80% of UNL is controlled by one party, they can block transactions

RPCA is BFT-lite: tolerates up to 20% Byzantine validators within UNL — assumes trusted validator set

Validator Network

Ripple runs some validators but minority — XRPL Foundation, universities, financial institutions also run validators

UNL is configurable: each node operator chooses their own UNL — recommendation list from Ripple is default

~35 recommended validators form the default UNL — actual network has ~150+ validators total

Network health: dUNL (decentralized UNL) project works to diversify validator set globally
Trust Lines
XRP Ledger's token system — IOUs and balances

2 XRP/line
Reserve

1,000+
Tokens

What Is a Trust Line

A trust line is a bidirectional credit relationship between two XRPL accounts

Explicitly trust an issuer (e.g., Bitstamp for USD) at a maximum limit you're willing to hold

Balance is stored in the trust line: positive = you hold issuer's IOU, negative = you owe issuer

Trust lines cost 2 XRP reserve per line — important for wallet minimum balance calculation

Issued Currencies

Any XRPL account can issue any currency code (3-letter or 20-byte hex)

Stablecoins: RLUSD (Ripple's USD stablecoin), USDT, USDC bridged to XRPL via AMM bridges

Common tokens: SOLO, CORE, CSC — community tokens, liquidity varies greatly

Pathfinding: XRPL automatically finds multi-hop paths through trust lines to make cross-currency payments

Rippling & No-Ripple

Rippling: default behavior — a payment can flow through your trust line balance to reach another account

No-ripple flag: set on a trust line to prevent your balance from being used in third-party payments

Why no-ripple matters: without it, your USD IOU from Bitstamp could be exchanged for USD from a different issuer

Market makers use rippling; individual holders set no-ripple to avoid unexpected balance changes
Native DEX & AMM
Order book + AMM hybrid — XRPL's built-in exchange

atomic
Settlement

0.6%
Fee

Native Order Book

XRPL has a built-in decentralized exchange at the protocol level — no smart contracts needed

OfferCreate transaction: specify sell amount + buy amount = creates limit order on ledger

Auto-matching: when you send a payment, XRPL automatically routes through best offers — DEX is invisible to user

Pathfinding: find_path API returns optimal route through trust lines and DEX to convert currencies

AMM (Automated Market Maker)

XRPL AMM added in 2024 (XLS-30) — constant product formula x × y = k like Uniswap V2

Each AMM pool issues LP tokens representing share of pool — LPs earn 0.6% trading fee

Auction slot: one special position that receives 25% rebate on fees — winner bid decays over 24h

AMM + order book: payments can route through both simultaneously for best execution price

Atomic Swaps

Every XRPL transaction is atomic — multi-step payments either fully succeed or fully revert

Escrow + Conditional payments: enable trustless cross-chain atomic swaps (no smart contracts)

Payment channels: off-ledger micropayments with final on-ledger settlement — millions of micro-txs, one settlement

DeFi on XRPL: no EVM but native DEX, AMM, escrow, payment channels cover most use cases
On-Demand Liquidity (ODL)
RippleNet's cross-border payment rails using XRP as bridge

30+
ODL corridors

seconds
Settlement

How ODL Works

Sender (e.g., US bank) buys XRP on US exchange → sends XRP to recipient country's exchange → recipient exchange sells XRP for local currency

Entire flow: ~3-5 seconds — vs 2-3 days for SWIFT wire transfer

No pre-funded accounts needed: eliminates nostro/vostro accounts banks maintain in each country

Current ODL corridors: US→Mexico, US→Philippines, US→EU, Australia→Philippines — 30+ active corridors

Why XRP?

XRP bridge: neutral asset neither sender nor recipient need to trust long-term — just for the 3 seconds of transfer

High liquidity: XRP is one of top 5 assets by volume — easy to buy/sell large amounts with low slippage

Low cost: XRP transaction fee = 0.00001 XRP (~$0.00001) — negligible vs SWIFT fees

Speed: 3-5 second finality required for ODL economics — slow finality = exchange rate risk

Competitive Landscape

SWIFT GPI: improved SWIFT with tracking — still 1-2 days, high fees

Stellar (XLM): competitor network with similar design — focused on financial inclusion, smaller volumes

Circle/USDC on multiple chains: stablecoin cross-border — removes FX risk but less liquidity in emerging markets

CBDC networks: future competition — if central banks issue CBDCs, direct interbank CBDC transfer possible
XRPL Pathfinding
How the protocol routes multi-hop payments

up to 6
Hops

auto-found
Paths

Path Discovery

ripple_path_find API: finds available paths from source currency to destination currency

Each path is a series of trust lines and order book offers that connect currencies

Example: send EUR, recipient receives SGD → find EUR→XRP→SGD path via two DEX trades

No-code routing: wallets call pathfinding, user just sees final quote — complexity hidden

Path Execution

Payment transaction includes found paths as fallback options — protocol uses best available

Partial payment flag: allow transaction to deliver less than requested (useful for variable conversion rate)

Slippage: if price moves between pathfinding and submission, transaction may fail — client retries

DEX + AMM: path can combine both — part of trade via order book, part via AMM pool

Optimization

Composite paths: XRPL can split payment across multiple paths simultaneously for best rate

Maximum path length: 6 hops — longer paths consume more transaction compute

Order book liquidity: deep ETH/XRP + XRP/USDC markets enable efficient multi-hop settlement

Auto-bridging: XRP automatically used as bridge currency between any two currencies — no explicit path needed
Why OnlyCrypto Runs on XRPL
Technical and business reasons for choosing XRPL

$0.00001
Fee

3-5 sec
Finality

Technical Advantages

Transaction cost: ~$0.00001 per transaction — vs $0.50-$5 on Ethereum even after L2s

Finality: 3-5 second final settlement — no probabilistic confirmation, no reorgs possible

Native token support: issue and transfer custom tokens without smart contracts — simpler, lower attack surface

No smart contract risk: XRPL transactions are predefined types — no arbitrary code execution bugs

Business Model Fit

Micropayments: $0.00001 fee enables per-lesson payments, micro-subscriptions, reward distributions

Membership payments: $99-$249 membership in XRP — instant settlement, no chargeback fraud

Trader profit distribution: tiny fees enable daily/weekly profit sharing without gas cost eating profits

Affiliate payouts: multi-level commission structure — XRPL batch payments send to 7 levels simultaneously

XRPL Ecosystem

XRPL EVM sidechain: brings EVM smart contracts to XRPL ecosystem — bridge to Ethereum DeFi

XRPL Grants program: $250M fund for XRPL ecosystem development — grants available for builders

Hooks (proposed): native smart contract-like functionality directly on XRPL — lightweight, auditable

Growing adoption: MoneyGram, Bitpay, BNY Mellon — institutional validation of XRPL's capabilities
XRPL Developer Guide
Accounts, transactions, and building on the ledger

npm install
xrpl.js

JSON-RPC + WS
API

Getting Started

xrpl.js (JavaScript), xrpl-py (Python), xrpl4j (Java) — official client libraries

Testnet: connect to wss://s.altnet.rippletest.net:51233 — free test XRP from Testnet Faucet

Create account: generate keypair → fund with minimum 10 XRP reserve → account activated

Explorer: xrpl.org/explorer — view ledger, transactions, accounts, DEX activity in real-time

Key Transaction Types

Payment: send XRP or IOU — core building block, most common transaction

TrustSet: establish or modify a trust line — required before holding any issued currency

OfferCreate/OfferCancel: place/cancel DEX orders — create liquidity on native exchange

EscrowCreate/EscrowFinish: time-lock or condition-lock XRP — trustless delayed payments

Building DeFi on XRPL

AMM pool creation: AMMCreate transaction — specify both assets and fee percentage

AMM deposit/withdraw: AMMDeposit/AMMWithdraw — receive/redeem LP tokens proportionally

NFT (XLS-20): NFTokenMint, NFTokenOffer, NFTokenAcceptOffer — native NFT standard, no smart contracts

Payment channels: PaymentChannelCreate/Claim — off-ledger micropayments for streaming payments
